title: anthemis rigida subsp. antri-neptuni
intro: Anthemis rigida subsp. antri-neptuni, also known as the mountain chamomile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis rigida plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Anthemis rigida subsp. antri-neptuni include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and providing relief from menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for women's health. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
